name: El Retiro Park
address: Plaza de la Independencia, Madrid, Spain
reason: Madrid's beautiful royal gardens - stroll to Crystal Palace and rent rowboats on the lake. 10 min walk from Hotel Fénix

name: Museo del Prado
address: Calle Ruiz de Alarcón 23, Madrid, Spain
reason: World-class Spanish masters - Velázquez and Goya's Black Paintings. No photography allowed. Book tickets online!

name: Cuesta de Moyano Book Market
reason: Charming outdoor book kiosks right next to the Prado - browse rare books and vintage finds

name: Joselito's Charcutería
reason: Premium jamón ibérico and charcuterie with wine. 5 min from Hotel Fénix in Salamanca

name: El Rastro Flea Market
reason: Sunday-only flea market 9am-3pm. Bring cash! Explore side streets for best antique finds

name: Living Retro by Judith San Quintín
reason: Art Deco & mid-century Italian pieces inside El Rastro market. Stunning 1950s/60s furniture

name: Pum Pum Bakery
address: Calle de la Esgrima 1, Madrid, Spain
reason: Excellent pastries and coffee for brunch. 3 min walk from El Rastro

name: Royal Palace of Madrid
reason: Largest royal palace in Western Europe. Book tickets online! Allow 2-2.5 hours

name: Temple of Debod
reason: Ancient Egyptian temple at sunset - stunning views. Book free entry online!

name: Divorare
reason: Focaccia sandwiches in Chueca - viral mortadella with stracciatella and pesto

name: Kohi Matcha Café
reason: Best matcha in Madrid - specialty flavors like Cloud or Mango. Grab a window seat

name: Tiny Big Sister
reason: Curated vintage women's clothing. Excellent service and unique pieces

name: Laagam
address: Calle San Vicente Ferrer, Madrid, Spain
reason: One of Malasaña's best indie boutiques - carefully curated vintage and contemporary

name: Despacio Concept Store
reason: Curated contemporary Spanish fashion & lifestyle - HAY and DOIY items

name: My Pasta Room
reason: Build-your-own pasta in a stunning art-filled space. Arrive early or book ahead

name: Bluelow
reason: Sustainable fashion and beautiful phone bag accessories. Opens 4pm Mondays

name: 99 Cheesecake
reason: Viral cheesecake spot - generous toppings for under €2. Closed Mondays, open from noon Tues-Sun

name: Restaurante Armando
reason: Huge portions, excellent escalope with raclette. No reservations - arrive early to avoid wait

name: Judith San Quintín Antiques
address: Calle Núñez de Balboa 15, Madrid, Spain
reason: Luxury Art Deco antiques & furniture in Salamanca. Exquisite taste and collector pieces

name: Loewe Flagship Store
reason: Iconic Spanish luxury leather goods in stunning architectural space

name: WOW Concept Store
reason: Curated multi-brand fashion & design. 2 min walk from Loewe

name: Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum
reason: 700 years of art - photography allowed. Free Mondays 12-4pm

name: Zara Gran Vía Flagship
reason: Largest Zara in Spain - Spanish high street fashion at its best

name: Museo Reina Sofía
reason: Picasso's Guernica, Dalí & Miró. Photography allowed. Open until 9pm - evening visit means fewer crowds

name: Chocolatería San Ginés
address: Pasadizo de San Ginés 5, Madrid, Spain
reason: Iconic churros con chocolate since 1894. Open 24 hours. Go early morning to avoid crowds

name: La Latina Tapas District
reason: Cobblestone streets with vermouth bars and pintxos. Best first evening in Madrid

name: Tiki Taco
reason: Casual Mexican spot for a quick lunch

name: Papizza
reason: Quick and casual pizza near Gran Vía

name: Malvón Bakery
reason: Quality pastries and coffee for brunch

name: Nudes Smoothies
reason: Healthy breakfast bowls and smoothies in Salamanca neighborhood

name: Hotel Fénix Gran Meliá
reason: Your hotel March 14-17. Salamanca neighborhood - 10 min to Retiro, 15 min to Prado

name: Four Seasons Hotel Madrid
reason: Your hotel March 18-23. Centro location - 5 min to Gran Vía, close to Reina Sofía
